episodes.title: Epstein Investigation: Attorney Mary David Explains Challenges Behind Prosecuting Human-Trafficking Networks | Crime House 24/7
episodes.description: Welcome to Crime House 24/7. Vanessa Richardson sits down with attorney and human-trafficking expert Mary David for an extended discussion about the Jeffrey Epstein investigation — often referred to as “the Epstein Files.” In this special segment, Mary explains how federal prosecutors alleged Epstein’s sex-trafficking operation functioned, the legal elements required to prove trafficking crimes, and the challenges investigators face when building cases involving grooming, recruitment, and exploitation of minors. Plus, the two examine how depositions and testimony from individuals connected to Epstein can help investigators understand the scope of his network and the broader implications for trafficking investigations moving forward. If you’re new here, don’t forget to follow Crime House 24/7 to never miss a case! For ad-free listening subscribe to Crime House+ on Apple Podcasts. episodes.title: Mother Found Not Guilty by Reason of Insanity in Son’s Drowning | Crime House 24/7
episodes.description: Vanessa Richardson brings you the latest breaking true crime stories. An Ohio judge has found an Amish mother not guilty by reason of insanity in the drowning death of her four-year-old son at Atwood Lake — a case investigators say unfolded during a shared spiritual delusion that also led to the drowning death of her husband. In Utah, a missing persons investigation has turned into a homicide case after a woman’s body was discovered inside her husband’s camper at a storage facility, while the husband fled the state and is now considered a person of interest. In Arkansas, a man charged with murdering the alleged abuser of his teenage daughter has won the Republican primary for county sheriff while awaiting trial. And in New York, singer Justin Timberlake is suing to block the release of police body camera footage from his 2024 drunken driving arrest.
